chunk_summary: This section outlines child data protection and app privacy requirements for tele-nutrition in India, emphasizing parental consent, safeguards against harmful practices, and compliance with data retention and erasure laws.
learning_objectives: Understand the regulatory requirements for processing child digital data in tele-nutrition platforms in India.
Identify prohibited practices such as targeted advertising and behavioral tracking in pediatric tele-nutrition apps.
Learn how to implement parental consent mechanisms and data retention schedules for child-specific safeguards.
key_facts: Under India's Digital Personal Data Protection framework, a child is defined as under 18 years, requiring verifiable parental consent for data processing.
Prohibited practices include tracking, behavioral monitoring, and targeted advertising directed at children.
Retention and erasure of child data must align with purpose justification and legal obligations, ensuring data minimization.
Tele-nutrition tools must avoid harmful nudges like rapid weight-loss prompts or stigmatizing notifications.
Free apps monetized via advertising SDKs or device-level tracking pose legal and ethical risks in pediatric care.
reference_quote: Under India’s Digital Personal Data Protection framework, a child is under 18 years and verifiable parental consent is required before processing a child’s personal data. (Source: https://www.meity.gov.in/static/uploads/2024/06/2bf1f0e9f04e6fb4f8fef35e82c42aa5.pdf)
document_key_topics: Prohibited practices in pediatric digital tools
